text.title: semantic-tokens-and-modes Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for semantic-tokens-and-modes as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent minting new reference colors instead of reusing existing palette when adding a token.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reference token proliferation defeats the palette abstraction. Lint must reject reference tokens that duplicate existing palette entries within a configurable delta.

failure-mode.description: Agent duplicating all tokens when asked to add a brand mode instead of overlaying only the delta values.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Full duplication creates N copies of every token, each requiring independent maintenance. A mode overlay contains only the tokens that differ from the base mode.

failure-mode.description: Style Dictionary configs generated by agents missing platform-specific transforms (e.g., size/pxToPt for iOS).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without the iOS pxToPt transform, point values are treated as pixel values, producing layout bugs that are only visible on physical devices — not in simulators.

failure-mode.description: Agent writing CSS like var(--color-blue-500) directly in component files.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Direct reference-token usage in components bypasses the semantic layer and breaks on theme switch. Lint rule must redirect to the semantic equivalent (e.g., var(--action-primary)).
